rationale: The synthetic determinism check requires concrete equality on `epmap` fields of `external_body` `HashMap<V>` (no `#[verifier::ext_equal]` attribute), but the spec of the external_body `receive_real_packet_impl` only constrains the abstract view via `SingleDelivery::receive_real_packet(...)`. Two distinct concrete HashMap values can share the same abstract view, so `post1_self_.receive_state.epmap == post2_self_.receive_state.epmap` is not derivable from view equality. Verified the implication-style postcondition reduces to two `epmap == epmap` assertions on opaque HashMap values; Verus reports `assertion failed` (no axioms relating view to structural equality for external_body HashMap). Attempt 1 of the prior run (attempt_01) tried the same case-split-then-extensional-equality strategy and failed in the identical way. Without introducing an `assume(...)` or a new lemma item (both forbidden by the task), the obligation is unprovable.
